summary.intent: Address Codex round-6 code review findings on the ColorPicker component, including a deeper architectural gap (missing on_open_change notification) and three bounded bugs (hue echo, RTL drag, hue-after-RGB).
summary.outcome: All 5 findings resolved: added on_open_change prop + Effect::OpenChange mirroring popover/dialog convention, fixed RTL drag x-inversion, hue echo on controlled sync, and hue refresh after RGB edits. 111 tests pass, spec synced, committed and pushed.

repo: Library overlay-sibling convention (popover, dialog) uses `open: Option<bool>` + `on_open_change` callback + `Effect::OpenChange` (optimistic flip + notify). ColorPicker was the only component missing this pattern.
The spec markdown formatter re-wraps `related:` frontmatter when the Edit tool is used, requiring a separate script pass to collapse it back.
Insta snapshot orphan checks and a coverage gate (separate Clippy run) are part of the standard pre-push validation pipeline.
Codex review threads are named with IDs (e.g. M5Pc, NNw8, NNxE, NNxG, NNw-) and the review cycle is iterative — unresolved threads from prior rounds persist alongside new findings each round.

code.finding: Effect enum was missing an OpenChange variant; added to fire on every open/close and booted-open, matching popover/dialog siblings.
apply_drag_position did not mirror physical x (1 − x) in RTL for area and channel drags, causing inverted drag behavior in RTL layouts.
set_channel_value did not refresh hue_value after RGB channel edits, leaving the displayed hue stale after indirect hue recomputation.
SyncValue arm was re-deriving hue even when the controlled value echoed the pending value, which destroyed the 360° hue endpoint. Fixed by skipping re-derive on echo (same pattern as ColorArea).

Spec needed updates in Props table (on_open_change), Effect enum, open_lifecycle_effects/close_plan helpers, apply_drag_position RTL note, set_channel_value RGB hue note, SyncValue echo guard, and §6 parity tables.

workflow: When a spec formatter hook re-wraps content after Edit-tool changes, applying a targeted fixup script after all edits are done avoids repeated reformatting cycles.
Checking the library convention in sibling components (popover, dialog) before implementing a fix revealed the root cause behind two seemingly separate review threads (M5Pc + NNw8).
Running compile + tests before writing new tests confirms no existing test pinned effect counts, reducing the risk of false test failures from additive changes.

friction: Codex did not reply to the M5Pc pushback from round 5, forcing re-examination of the thread alongside 4 new findings without explicit acknowledgment.
The spec markdown formatter re-wrapped `related:` frontmatter on every Edit-tool save, requiring a separate script pass to fix — a recurring annoyance in spec editing.
NNw8's stated ask (strict controlled semantics) conflicted with the established library convention, requiring a judgment call and an explanation to document in the thread reply.
open_items: NNw8 strict-controlled semantics (don't mutate open state while open.is_some()) was intentionally not implemented in favor of matching the optimistic-flip+on_open_change convention of popover/dialog siblings. A library-wide strict-controlled mode remains a possible future change.
Thread replies and resolution for M5Pc, NNw8, NNxE, NNxG, NNw- need to be posted after push, and Codex re-triggered for round 7.
